title: chelidonium grandiflorum
intro: Chelidonium grandiflorum, also known as Greater Celandine, is a flowering plant in the poppy family (Papaveraceae) native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects, as well as its ability to stimulate the immune system. The health benefits of Chelidonium grandiflorum include treating skin conditions such as warts and eczema, as well as digestive issues like irritable bowel syndrome and ulcers. Chelidonium grandiflorum can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or salve, and is also used in homeopathic remedies to promote overall well-being.
